title: Tools: Giving AI Hands
summary: An LLM alone mainly produces text - it cannot browse, calculate reliably, or send an email by itself. Tool calling is the loop that lets it interact with external systems and become more practically useful.

context.headline: A brilliant brain gets hands
context.description: On its own, a language model can only generate text. Tool calling closes that gap: the model requests an action, the surrounding app performs it, and the result flows back into the conversation. This loop lets a text model interact with search, code, files, and other systems.
context.when: The step from talking to doing
